nodes.title: Character Card Segment Inclusion Matrix
widgets_values:
| SEGMENT           | ADDITIONAL CONDITION             | TRIGGER TAGS (only if condition is fulfilled)                              | TRIGGER TAGS (condition-independent) | COMMENT                             |
|-------------------|----------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
| CHARACTER         |                                  |                                                                            |                                      | always included                     |
| HEAD              |                                  | wide_shot/full_body/feet_out_of_frame/cowboy_shot/upper_body/portrait/face |                                      |                                     |
| EYEBALLS          | NOT (facing_away OR closed_eyes) | wide_shot/full_body/feet_out_of_frame/cowboy_shot/upper_body/portrait/face |                                      | tags to hide if eyes closed         |
| TONGUE            |                                  | any tag contains tongue or licking                                         |                                      | not included if part of other word  |
| FACE              | NOT facing_away                  | wide_shot/full_body/feet_out_of_frame/cowboy_shot/upper_body/portrait/face | FACE                                 | include things like front bangs too |
| PORTRAIT_FRONT    | NOT from_behind                  | wide_shot/full_body/feet_out_of_frame/cowboy_shot/upper_body/portrait      | PORTRAIT                             |                                     |
| PORTRAIT_BACK     | from_behind                      | wide_shot/full_body/feet_out_of_frame/cowboy_shot/upper_body/portrait      | PORTRAIT                             |                                     |
| UPPER_BODY_FRONT  | NOT from_behind                  | wide_shot/full_body/feet_out_of_frame/cowboy_shot/upper_body               | UPPER_BODY                           |                                     |
| UPPER_BODY_BACK   | from_behind                      | wide_shot/full_body/feet_out_of_frame/cowboy_shot/upper_body               | UPPER_BODY                           |                                     |
| COWBOY_SHOT_FRONT | NOT from_behind                  | wide_shot/full_body/feet_out_of_frame/cowboy_shot                          | COWBOY_SHOT                          |                                     |
| COWBOY_SHOT_BACK  | from_behind                      | wide_shot/full_body/feet_out_of_frame/cowboy_shot                          | COWBOY_SHOT                          |                                     |
| FEET_OUT_OF_FRAME |                                  | wide_shot/full_body/feet_out_of_frame                                      | FEET_OUT_OF_FRAME/legs               |                                     |
| FULL_BODY         |                                  | wide_shot/full_body                                                        | FULL_BODY/legs/LEGS/feet             |                                     |
| HANDS             | NOT tag contains pov             | any tag contains hand(s)                                                   |                                      | not included if part of other word  |
| TAIL              | from_behind                      | wide_shot/full_body/feet_out_of_frame/cowboy_shot/upper_body               | any tag contains tail                | not included if part of other word  |
| BACK WINGS        | from_behind                      | wide_shot/full_body/feet_out_of_frame/cowboy_shot/upper_body               | any tag contains wing(s)             | not included if part of other word  |
| NEGATIVE          |                                  |                                                                            |                                      | always included as negative tags    |

NOTES:
- essentially, for a segmant to be included you want the 2nd AND 3rd column to be true or you want the 4th column to be true. If CONDITION is empty it is automatically true
- everything is case-sensitive but underscores (_) may be replaced with spaces ( )
    - lower_case will implicitly include segments of narrower framing consistent with booru tagging, eg; `upper_body` will include `upper_body`, `portrait` and `face` segments of the character card
    - UPPER_CASE will only ever include the specified segment, eg; `UPPER_BODY` will only include the `upper_body` segment of the character card.
- conditions are used to additionally check if certain segments should be visible, for example if the character is facing_away, the face segment will not be included. If they have their eyes_closed, then the eyeballs will not be included.
- hands can be in all sorts of different positions and therefore require explicit mention in the main prompt
- tail and back wings vary in size and shape and may not be visible from the front. Viewed from front, they require explicit mention in the main prompt.
- for hands, it is enough that one qualifying trigger tag fulfills the condition
- there are some cases with too much ambiguity such as 'head_out_of_frame' referring to both excluding the entire head or just half from eyes and up. In those cases manual editing of the tags in the character card is sadly necessary

About

Generate an image with multiple characters while keeping every character's prompt separate to avoid contamination. OpenPose ControlNet is used to ensure consistency in the image.

Requirements

OpenPose ControlNet model compatible with your Checkpoint architecture. The detailer models in Group III should download automatically on first run, if not you either need to get them manually or just remove the detailer nodes as they're not core to this workflow's functionality. You need the following Custom Node Packs from the Custom Node Manager:
  1. ComfyUI-Custom-Scripts
  2. ComfyUI Impact Pack
  3. ComfyUI Impact Subpack
  4. ComfyUI Inspire Pack
  5. comfyui_controlnet_aux
  6. WAS Node Suite (Revised)
Recommended Custom Node Packs:
  1. rgthree-comfy - adds toggles to groups which is great QoL for this workflow

How to Use

  1. Disable Group II and Group III
  2. Generate an image in Group I:
    • The purpose of this image is to be analyzed by an OpenPose Preprocessor. You want to get the number of characters for the final image and desired poses. This is not the final image, it is discarded after pose data is extracted for the next step.
    • Repeat this step until you get the poses you want and the OpenPose Preprocessor is able to extract the pose data correctly enough.
  3. Disable Group I1 and enable Group II
  4. Run the workflow. You will get an error. Close it. An image should appear on the leftmost Preview Bridge node in Group II.
  5. Right-Click the image on the Preview Bridge and Open in Mask Editor.
  6. Draw a mask for one of the characters following the pose data overlay.
  7. Repeat from Step 4 for every character.
  8. After every character's mask has been drawn, you can enable Group III
  9. Run the workflow to generate the final image

Number of Characetrs

You can have an arbitrary number of characters. Simply add as many LLC Subject subgraphs along with corresponding Preview Bridge and Character Card nodes and place them to the right. Copy over the I/O links from the rightmost set of nodes. To change the color of the mask on preview images, open the corresponding LAC Subject subgraph node and edit

Character Cards

The character card system is completely optional. If a node accepts a character_card input, it must be connected, but you can just leave it empty and use the regular prompt boxes as normal. You can fold the Character Card nodes (by clicking on the dot in the top left of the node) to save space. If you decide to give it a try; The Character Card system is designed to enable easy reuse of characters by segregating tags into segments the inclusion of which is decided by the contents of the main prompt box. This gets rid of the need to constantly rewrite your prompts by adding and removing tags describing a character's features depending on the desired framing or actions. By design it should be as natural and seamless as possible to use without thinking about it and from experience it achieves that remarkably well but as the designer I am definitely biased. The rules and some reasoning behind them are thoroughly explained in the Character Card Segment Inclusion Matrix below this node. An empty character card should be to the right of the matrix. This system is designed to work with booru-style tags and will struggle with natural prompting.

Quality Prompt Nodes

These nodes supply some common quality tags like (masterpiece:1.4) so that the regular prompt boxes you use can be kept clean. If you do not wish to use them, just select option 0 which will not add any tags. You can inspect the contents of these prompts, modify them and add new ones by opening the subgraph and edit the values of nodes in the Color group. Replace_Red/Green/Blue control the color itself and blend_percentage on the second node controls the opacity. Those changes are purely visual intended as QoL visual help and have no effect on the final image.

Size Selector Node

This node allows you to quickly switch between common resolutions in sets of 5 with an ability to flip between portrait and landscape mode which simply flips the axes. Inspect by opening the subgraph. By default it is set to a custom size as it is unintuitive at first.

Basic Pipe (-)

Basic Pipe (-) links present on the LLC Subject nodes are the same as the regular Basic Pipe but do not contain the LoRA hooks or ControlNet guidance. This is because both of these have high negative impact on sampling times, especially after upscaling but are not necessary after the first round of sampling. By default Basic Pipe (with LoRA and ControlNet) is used before upscaling and Basic Pipe (-) (no LoRA and no ControlNet) is used after to speed up sampling.

Groups I and III

The core of this workflow is the composition of conditionings in Group II. The other two groups are not required for the core functionality and as such can be replaced with something else. You can input your own prepared pose images instead of creating them dynamically in Group I or modify the way the final image is generated in Group III however you like.

Masking Tips

  • Outstretched limbs (eg. in hugs) need fairly generous masks, especially when wearing thick sleeves.
  • Some overlap between masks is fine, but if a character A's arm is in front of characer B, you may want to avoid completely painting over A's arm with B's mask to avoid A's limb fusing into B. If it is still happening, expand A's mask around the limb.
  • If both character's faces have been detected as one, this can still work. If you mask one eye/ear node for each character and include the nose node for both you'll get them facing each other in a profile view. This will work for something like kissing.
  • In general you'll just have to experiment how much weirdness in the pose detection you can get away with before the final image will start breaking down. Even if parts on the pose data image are missing or are a bit wacky, the model in the final generation can fill the gaps somewhat
